pi_items.source: Task description specifies these output files; setup_task.sh removes them at line 21-22; export_result.sh checks for their existence at lines 14-15 and 28-29

Verified through multiple sources: (1) MEWS is a Modified Early Warning Score with 5 parameters including consciousness level assessed by AVPU scale (https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/1875/modified-early-warning-score-mews-clinical-deterioration); (2) AVPU cannot be automated by standard sensors - requires manual clinical assessment (https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S073567572300517X); (3) Multiparameter Monitor and Pulse Oximeter are confirmed as available simulated device types in OpenICE (verified in create_simulated_device and view_device_vitals task descriptions)

Task description explicitly requires three devices: (1) Multiparameter Monitor (for HR/BP/RR), (2) Pulse Oximeter (for SpO2), (3) Temperature-capable device. Verifier.py (line 84-96) counts devices found and awards points for each up to 3. OpenICE supports these device types as confirmed in simulated_device_parameter_exploration task metadata and openice_architecture_assessment task description

pi_items.metadata_value: AVPU/consciousness assessment is the parameter that cannot be automated
pi_items.verified_value: AVPU/consciousness is the only MEWS parameter that cannot be fully automated by standard medical sensors - it requires manual clinical assessment by healthcare professionals
pi_items.source: Web search on MEWS parameters (https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/1875/modified-early-warning-score-mews-clinical-deterioration) confirms MEWS has 5 parameters: systolic BP, heart rate, respiratory rate, temperature, and consciousness level. The first 4 can be measured by sensors (Multiparameter Monitor and Pulse Oximeter), but consciousness/AVPU (Alert, Voice-responsive, Pain-responsive, Unresponsive) requires manual observation and stimulation by healthcare professionals (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK538431/). Research shows attempts to automate AVPU using video/speech recognition exist but are not standard clinical practice

pi_items.metadata_value: MEWS measures heart rate, systolic blood pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, and consciousness
pi_items.verified_value: MEWS (Modified Early Warning Score) consists of 5 physiological parameters: (1) Heart rate, (2) Systolic blood pressure, (3) Respiratory rate, (4) Temperature, (5) Level of consciousness (AVPU). Overall score ranges from 0-14, with score ≥5 indicating increased risk of deterioration
pi_items.source: Verified through multiple authoritative medical sources: (https://www.mdcalc.com/calc/1875/modified-early-warning-score-mews-clinical-deterioration) and (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3901724/). Web searches confirm the exact 5 parameters and scoring thresholds

pi_items.metadata_value: Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, Temperature device types are available
pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE supports simulated device types including: Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, Temperature, Capnograph, Infusion Pump, Non-Invasive Blood Pressure (NIBP), ECG, IBP
pi_items.source: Verified in task.json files: (1) create_simulated_device task explicitly creates 'Multiparameter Monitor' (lines 5, 18); (2) view_device_vitals task confirms Multiparameter Monitor displays HR/BP/SpO2 (line 5); (3) simulated_device_parameter_exploration task lists expected_device_types including Multiparameter Monitor, Capnograph, Pulse Oximeter, Infusion Pump, Non-Invasive Blood Pressure (lines 20-26); (4) openice_architecture_assessment task lists known types including 'Temperature' (line 2). OpenICE is a real platform from MD PnP/MGH (https://mdpnp.mgh.harvard.edu/astra-portfolio/openice/)

pi_items.metadata_value: Vital Signs is a clinical application in OpenICE
pi_items.verified_value: Vital Signs is a clinical demonstration application in OpenICE that displays vital signs data from connected devices
pi_items.source: Web search results confirm OpenICE has a 'Vital Signs' clinical application (https://www.openice.info/vitals.html). Task descriptions reference launching 'Vital Signs' app (adverse_event_rca_reconstruction task). OpenICE Supervisor can run demonstration applications including Vital Signs (https://www.openice.info/docs/3_apps.html)

privileged_info_summary: MEWS (Modified Early Warning Score) is a validated clinical scoring system with 5 parameters: systolic blood pressure, heart rate, respiratory rate, temperature, and consciousness (assessed via AVPU scale). The task requires creating three OpenICE simulated medical devices: a Multiparameter Monitor (measures HR, BP, RR), a Pulse Oximeter (measures SpO2), and a Temperature device. The Vital Signs clinical application is available in OpenICE to visualize device data. Critically, AVPU/consciousness assessment is the only MEWS parameter that cannot be automated by standard medical sensors - it requires manual clinical observation and assessment by healthcare professionals, which is the key automation gap the feasibility assessment must identify.
